data.title: 180 Degree Capital reports NAV of $4.64 as of December 31
data.description:
180 Degree Capital (NASDAQ:TURN) Friday reported Net Asset Value Per Share of $4.64 as of December 31, 2024.

“While our full-year performance was disappointing, Q1 2025 has thus far continued and exceeded our strong performance exiting 2024," Kevin M. Rendino, Chief Executive Officer of 180 Degree Capital said.

"Our gross total return of +205% from inception through the end of 2024 continues to compare favorably to the +69% total return for the Russell Microcap Index."

data.title: Moderna GAAP EPS of -$2.91 misses by $0.15, revenue of $966M beats by $24.86M
data.description:
• Moderna press release (NASDAQ:MRNA): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$2.91 misses by $0.15.
  • Loss includes approximately $0.2 billion of non-cash charges related to manufacturing resizing.
  • Expects 2025 revenue range of $1.5 to 2.5 billion andending cash balance of approximately $6 billion
  • Revenue of $966M (-65.6% Y/Y) beats by $24.86M.

data.title: Essent Group GAAP EPS of $1.58 misses by $0.08, revenue of $315.03M beats by $1.15M
data.description:
• Essent Group press release (NYSE:ESNT): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.58 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $315.03M (+6.0% Y/Y) beats by $1.15M.
  • New insurance written for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $12.2 billion, compared to $12.5 billion in the third quarter of 2024 and $8.8 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Insurance in force as of December 31, 2024 was $243.6 billion, compared to $243.0 billion as of September 30, 2024 and $239.1 billion as of December 31, 2023.

data.title: MTY Food Group reports Q4 results
data.description:
• MTY Food Group press release (OTCPK:MTYFF): Q4 Net loss attributable to owners of $55.3 million, or $2.34 per diluted share
  • Revenue of $284.46M (+1.6% Y/Y).
  • Normalized adjusted EBITDA remained relatively stable at $59.4 million in the quarter, compared to $60.4 million in Q4-23.
  • System sales for the quarter improved by 2% or $30.3 million to reach $1,371.9 million compared to $1,341.6 million in Q4-23 mostly due to organic growth.
  • Ended the quarter with 7079 locations with net positive openings of 13 locations for the quarter.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Trade tensions, adverse weather to provide support for agricultural softs, BMI says
data.description:
Soft agricultural goods will find support as market participants brace for potential disruptions in trade flow, BMI Research said, as sugar and coffee continue to lead the gains.

On the one hand, persistent production challenges are exerting upward pressure on global coffee and sugar prices (SB1:COM), as adverse weather conditions in Brazil continue to drive coffee prices higher, while similar production issues in major sugar-producing countries, such as Brazil and India, are bolstering sugar prices, the brokerage said.

“On the other hand, while escalating trade tensions introduce a downside risk to grain prices—primarily due to the threat of retaliatory tariffs impacting U.S. exports—the same tensions have bolstered prices for soft agricultural commodities, such as coffee and sugar.”

BMI attributes this to the United States' substantial dependency on import volumes for these commodities, where increased uncertainty regarding potential further tariff announcements has reinforced price support.

Coffee Futures (KC1:COM) are up 37% so far this year, and 124% over the past one-year amid supply and tariff concerns.

Sugar futures (SB1:COM) are meanwhile up 4.3% for the week and have gained 4.5% YTD.

Fitch's BMI further argued that while the escalating trade tensions are expected to negatively impact grain prices—primarily due to the risk of retaliatory tariffs affecting U.S. soybean exports to Canada and China—the scenario differs for soft agricultural commodities like coffee and sugar, for which the U.S. maintains a trade deficit in these products.

Given this context, it believes that persistent threats of tariffs by President Trump will bolster and support prices for these commodities.

“Even though the tariffs against Colombian imports were withdrawn, current market prices still reflect the heightened risk of tariffs being imposed on markets where the US relies heavily on import volumes. This perception of risk, combined with ongoing trade tensions, leads us to expect that prices for soft agricultural goods will continue to find support as market participants brace for potential disruptions in trade flows.”

CBOT corn (C_1:COM) +0.62% to $496.58 per bushel, soybeans (S_1:COM) +0.87% to $1,038.99 per bushel, and wheat (W_1:COM) +1.74% to $587.81 by 6:04 am ET.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:CORN), (NYSEARCA:SOYB), (NYSEARCA:WEAT), (DBA), (NYSEARCA:MOO)

data.title: Trump mulls changes to US CHIPS Act terms - report
data.description:
The Trump administration is seeking to renegotiate awards under the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act awards and has indicated delays to some upcoming semiconductor disbursements, Reuters reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The CHIPS Act brought by the previous Biden administration provides for $39B in grants, billions more in loans and 25% tax credits to boost domestic chip manufacturing and reduce reliance on Asia. Many companies have received awards under the initiative.

The White House is now looking to review the projects awarded. The government plans to renegotiate some of the agreements after evaluating and changing current requirements. However, the extent of the possible changes, and how they could affect agreements already finalized, was not clear. It was also not known if any action has yet been taken, the report added.

“The CHIPS Program Office has told us that certain conditions that do not align with President (Donald) Trump's executive orders and policies are now under review for all CHIPS Direct Funding Agreements,” GlobalWafers's spokesperson Leah Peng told the news agency.

Taiwanese company GlobalWafers' CEO Doris Hsu said that the current status is that the CHIPS Act remains unchanged, and the company has secured contracts worth $406M, according to a separate report by Reuters.

The U.S. Department of Commerce and GlobalWafers did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

"However, at this moment, everything is hypothetical and hasn't happened yet, as we haven't received any notification about required actions. For now, everything is proceeding according to the original plan" Hsu said while speaking to reporters in Taiwan's Hsinchu, the report noted.

Hsu, noted the company has not yet received any funding from the U.S. because it needs to reach certain milestones this year.

"We have a first milestone to achieve, and once we reach it, we will submit the necessary documentation to the relevant authorities for review," Hsu said.

In December 2024, the U.S. Department of Commerce finalized up to $406M in funding for GlobalWafers under the CHIPS Act to ramp up domestic silicon wafer production.

GlobalWafers is not changing its U.S. expansion plan, which is ongoing at three facilities across the country, Hsu noted.

The White House is concerned about several terms underpinning the $39B CHIPS and Science Act industry subsidies, the report noted, citing sources.

These consists of additional clauses, including requirements added into contracts by the Biden administration which say that recipients must use unionized labor to set up factories and help provide affordable childcare for factory workers, the report added.

The White House is also concerned that companies that accepted the subsidies and then announced expansion plans abroad, including in China. The law allowed some investments in China, the report noted.

Intel (INTC), for instance, announced a $300M investment plan in a Chinese assembly and test facility in October 2024, after noting in March that it had won an award under the CHIPS Act.

In November 2025, The U.S. government finalized up to $7.86B in direct funding for Intel (INTC) under the CHIPS Act. Some of the other companies slated to receive funding under the CHIPS Act are Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM), Micron Technology (MU), HP Inc (HPQ), GlobalFoundries (GFS) has South Korean companies Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) and SK hynix.

Many recipients of the CHIPS Act funding have major manufacturing facilities in China, the report added.

Intel disclosed it had received two payments totaling $2.2B in funding under the CHIPS Act, but declined to comment, the report noted.

A spokesperson for TSM said the company received $1.5B monies before the new administration came in as per the milestone terms of its agreement. The spokesperson declined comment on any potential changes to its agreement under the new U.S. government but said the company is continuing to engage with the CHIPS Program Office, the report added.

TSM, Intel, Micron, HP Inc., GlobalFoundries, Samsung and SK hynix did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Portland General Electric reports FY results
data.description:
• Portland General Electric press release (NYSE:POR): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $3.14.
  • Revenue of $3.44B (+17.8% Y/Y).
  • Initiating 2025 adjusted earnings guidance of $3.13 to $3.33 per diluted share and reaffirming 5% to 7% long-term earnings per share growth using a base of $3.08 per diluted share, the mid-point of original 2024 adjusted earnings guidance
